summary: Independent verification of Feature 1 (FPS/game in telemetry) + Feature 2 (WebPush alerts on CPU/GPU temp) + regression on profiles. Created pytest suite test_alerts_fps.py (10 tests, all PASS): GET /api/alerts defaults+persistence, PUT /api/alerts persists cpu_max/gpu_max/enabled, POST /api/agent/telemetry with fps=144 game='valorant' surfaces in /api/pc-telemetry last sample and live=true, high gpu_temp=95 vs low gpu_max=80 returns {ok:true} without errors + cooldown branch (second call also 200 with no crash), invalid token returns 401, GET /api/agent/script?mode=monitor contains Start-Fps/Get-Fps/Stop-Fps, PresentMon-1.10.0-x64.exe URL, monitor branch with try/finally, and Token non valido fallback for bad token. Profiles regression: /api/profiles/templates has 5 templates + catalog, /api/profiles OK. Playwright E2E on public preview: login admin OK, /app/live shows all 8 stat cards (stat-fps..stat-gpu-power), alert-settings panel with alert-enabled/cpu-max/gpu-max/save-alerts-btn works — changing thresholds to 87/81 triggers success toast and values persist after reload. Regression: /app/profiles shows 5 preset cards, new-profile-btn present, nav-live and nav-profiles rendered.
critical_code_review_comments: push.send_push_to_user silently returns when VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY missing (correct for dev) — telemetry alert path is defensive with try/except so even push errors don't fail the endpoint. Good.
_check_temp_alerts always writes last_{metric}_alert even if push had no subscribers — cooldown still starts, which is intentional to avoid spamming.
AlertInput has no bounds validation on cpu_max/gpu_max; negative or absurd values (e.g. 0 or 500) are accepted. Consider Field(ge=40, le=110). Non-blocking.
Live.jsx: parseInt(e.target.value) || 0 falls back to 0 if user clears input — might be surprising but acceptable.
seed_data_creation: alert_settings for admin ended at cpu_max=87 gpu_max=81 (test data); telemetry sample with fps=144 game='valorant' appended (rotated by 60-sample cap).
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_alerts_fps.py is the regression suite for alerts + fps telemetry + monitor PS script. Run with `python -m pytest backend/tests/test_alerts_fps.py -o addopts=''` to avoid xdist parallel race on the shared admin alert_settings doc.
